title: Kerala Fish Molee

ingredients.name: seer fish (vanjaram) or pomfret, cut in steaks

subs.name: prawn (shrimp) for prawn moilee
subs.notes: cook prawns only 4–5 minutes or they turn rubbery

subs.name: thin coconut milk only throughout, with desiccated coconut added for body
subs.notes: avoid canned coconut cream which is too heavy

ingredients.name: thin coconut milk (second extract or diluted first)

subs.name: thin coconut milk only throughout, with desiccated coconut added for body
subs.notes: avoid canned coconut cream which is too heavy

subs.name: a small piece of kokum soaked in 2 tbsp water
subs.notes: adds a different but authentic Kerala sourness

steps.text: Rub the fish steaks with half the turmeric and a little salt. Set aside for 10 minutes while you prep the remaining ingredients.
Heat coconut oil in a wide heavy-bottomed pan on medium flame. Add cardamom, cloves and cinnamon and let them sizzle for 20 seconds until fragrant.
Add the sliced onion and cook for 4–5 minutes, stirring, until soft and pale gold but not browned. Add ginger, garlic and green chillies and saute for 2 minutes until the raw smell leaves.
Add the remaining turmeric and cracked black pepper. Stir for 30 seconds, then pour in the thin coconut milk. Add the tomato slices if using. Bring to a gentle simmer — do not let it boil hard.

Gently slide the marinated fish steaks into the simmering gravy. Cook uncovered on low-medium flame for 8–10 minutes, spooning the gravy over the fish occasionally, until the fish is cooked through — it should flake cleanly when pressed. Avoid turning the pieces more than once or they will break.

Reduce the flame to the lowest setting. Pour in the thick coconut milk and add the curry leaves. Swirl the pan gently to combine — do not stir hard. Heat for 2 minutes without letting the gravy boil, or the coconut milk will split.
Add lemon juice, check salt, and remove from heat. Serve with Kerala appam, idiyappam or plain steamed rice. The gravy should be pale yellow and gently fragrant.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Cook seafood until safely done for the species and preparation; keep it chilled before cooking.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: fish → prawn (shrimp) for prawn moilee (cook prawns only 4–5 minutes or they turn rubbery); coconut-milk → thin coconut milk only throughout, with desiccated coconut added for body (avoid canned coconut cream which is too heavy); lemon → a small piece of kokum soaked in 2 tbsp water (adds a different but authentic Kerala sourness)
